grammar_topics.title: Personal Pronouns
grammar_topics.description: Introduction to Arabic pronouns (ana, anta, huwa, hiya, nahnu, antum, hum)
grammar_topics.rule: Pronouns indicate the person performing or receiving the action

grammar_topics.title: Present Tense Basics
grammar_topics.rule: Present tense in Arabic is formed by modifying the root verb

grammar_topics.title: Definite Article (ال)
grammar_topics.description: Using the definite article 'al' (the) in Arabic
grammar_topics.rule: Add 'ال' to make a noun definite

grammar_topics.title: Gender and Number Agreement
grammar_topics.description: Adjectives and verbs must agree with nouns in gender and number
grammar_topics.rule: Masculine, feminine, singular, and plural forms must match

dialogues.title: First Meeting
dialogues.situation: Two people meeting for the first time

cultural_insights.title: Arabic Greetings
cultural_insights.description: The greeting 'السلام عليكم' (As-salamu alaikum) is the most common formal greeting in the Arab world. The response 'وعليكم السلام ورحمة الله وبركاته' emphasizes peace, mercy, and blessings.

cultural_insights.title: Importance of Respect
cultural_insights.description: Arabic language emphasizes respect through formal pronouns and honorific titles. Using the informal 'you' with elders or strangers is considered disrespectful.

cultural_insights.title: Religious References
cultural_insights.description: Many Arabic greetings and expressions include religious references like 'الحمدلله' (Praise be to God). These are integral to daily communication across the Arab world.

cultural_insights.title: Names and Introductions
cultural_insights.description: Arabic names often have meanings and come from a family or personal naming tradition. When introducing yourself, it's common to mention your father's name as well.

exercises.question: What is the correct response to 'السلام عليكم'?
exercises.explanation: The correct response acknowledges the greeting and wishes peace, mercy, and blessings.

exercises.question: Which pronoun means 'she'?
exercises.explanation: In Arabic, 'هي' (hiya) is the feminine third-person singular pronoun meaning 'she'.

exercises.question: What does 'الكتاب' mean?
exercises.explanation: The definite article 'ال' makes 'كتاب' (book) definite, meaning 'the book'.

exercises.question: Choose the sentence with correct gender agreement:
How do you say 'thank you' in Arabic?
exercises.explanation: 'شكرا' (Shukran) is the standard way to say thank you.

exercises.question: What is 'من فضلك'?
exercises.explanation: 'من فضلك' (min fadlik) means 'please' in a polite request.

exercises.question: Which pronoun means 'we'?
exercises.explanation: 'نحن' (nahnu) is the first-person plural pronoun meaning 'we'.

exercises.question: What does 'تمام' mean?
exercises.explanation: 'تمام' (tamam) means 'fine' or 'okay', indicating everything is well.

exercises.question: How do you ask 'How are you?' informally?
exercises.explanation: 'كيف حالك' (Kayf haluk) is the standard informal way to ask how someone is.

exercises.question: What is the proper formal greeting?
exercises.explanation: 'السلام عليكم' (As-salamu alaikum) is the most formal and respectful greeting.
